title: Defrost a C. elegans Strain from the -80 C Freezer
author: Cristian Riccio
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.5xug7nw
materials: Put the tube in a foam floater.

steps.title: Take the frozen C
steps.instruction: Take the frozen C. elegans tube out of the -80 C freezer.

steps.title: Put the foam floater on the...
steps.instruction: Put the foam floater on the surface of lukewarm water in a beaker.

steps.title: When the content of the tube...
steps.instruction: When the content of the tube has defrosted (should take only a few minutes), pour the contents onto an NGM plate seeded with bacteria.

steps.title: Put the plate in a plastic...
steps.instruction: Put the plate in a plastic box and put the box into an incubator, for example a 20 C incubator.

steps.title: The following day, check that some worms are alive
steps.instruction: The following day, check that some worms are alive. I would recommend having at least 60 living pre-reprodutive or reproductive worms on the plate.
